labels.label:
Convert JSON to AVRO: This processor converts the 
JSON response to AVRO format. It uses RecordReader 
and RecordWriters to accomplish this. It infers the JSON 
schema starting from the recent changes field.

Write To File: These processors write the filtered, routed 
edit events to two different locations on the local disk. 

In CDF-PC, you would typically not write to local disk 
but replace these processors with processors that 
resemble your destination system (Kafka, Database, 
Object Store etc.)

Route On Content Size: This processor uses two SQL 
statements to separate edit events that resulted in a 
longer article from edit events that resulted in a shorter 
article.

This flow retrieves the most recent changes from 
Wikipedia, converts the response from JSON to AVRO, 
filters out any changes that are not edits before routing 
edit events based on whether they added or removed 
content to the article. 

Ultimately this flow writes files to the local disk.

Filter Edits: This processor filters out anything but actual 
page edits. To achieve this, it's running a query that 
selects all FlowFiles (events) of the type "edit".

Merge Edit Events: These processors are configured to 
merge at least 100 records into one flowfile to avoid 
writing lots of small files.

The MaxBinAge property is set to 2 minutes which 
makes the processors merge records after two 
minutes even if less than 100 records have arrived.

Get Recent Wikipedia Changes: This processor calls 
the Wikipedia API to retrieve the latest changes. 

Explore its configuration to see how query parameters 
are passed in.
